title: Crumbly Light Greek Parma Ham Gyro
description: A light and crumbly Greek dish featuring parma ham, simmered in a nimono-style broth and wrapped in a gyro, with a bitter twist from arugula and a mediterranean flair from feta cheese.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Wrap the pita bread in foil and warm it in the oven for 5 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the sliced red onion.
Cook the onion for 5 minutes, or until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the diced tomato to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Brush the lemon juice mixture onto the warmed pita bread.
Assemble the gyro by spreading a layer of feta cheese on the pita bread, followed by a few slices of parma ham, some arugula, and finally the cooked onion and tomato mixture.
To add a nimono-simmered twist, simmer the parma ham in a broth made with olive oil, lemon juice, and spices for 10 minutes before assembling the gyro.
